text:
There once was a mouse named Whiskers who lived in a cozy hole near an ancient oak tree. He was known for his big brown eyes and sweet little nose. Every day after nibbling on some freshly ground cinnamon scrolls, Whiskers would climb down into the oak's branches to listen to the birds'.

Once, during one particular summer's day, he heard something peculiar. A song, he thought. But it wasn't just any song—with each note, it seemed, a story unfolded. First came a cheerful woodpecker, then a gentle nightingale. They sang of love and loneliness, of adventures lost and found, of laughter that echoed even in the quietest nights.

Whiskers listened intently. Perhaps he'd meet these musicians? He decided to visit the forest's edge. To his delight, there they were - a squirrel with a flute, a bear with a harp, and a wise old owl playing the oldest flute he'd ever heard. They welcomed
